title: Seviyan Vermicelli

seo.title: How to Make Seviyan Vermicelli — Easy Pakistani Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Seviyan Vermicelli. This easy Pakistani recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Seviyan Vermicelli — a beloved Pakistani classic

description: Seviyan Vermicelli is a delightful and versatile Pakistani dish made from thin roasted vermicelli noodles. Often served as a sweet dessert or a savory side, it offers a comforting texture and rich flavor. This recipe captures the essence of a cherished Pakistani classic, perfect for any occasion.

ingredients.notes: optional, soaked in 1 tbsp warm milk

instructions.title: Roast the Vermicelli
instructions.text: In a heavy-bottomed pot or large pan, melt the ghee over medium heat. Add the broken vermicelli and roast, stirring continuously, until it turns golden brown and fragrant. Be careful not to burn it. This should take about 3-5 minutes. Remove the roasted vermicelli from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Milk Mixture
instructions.text: In the same pot, add the milk, sugar, and lightly crushed green cardamom pods. Bring the mixture to a gentle boil over medium heat, stirring occasionally to dissolve the sugar completely. If using saffron, add it now.

instructions.title: Combine and Cook
instructions.text: Once the milk mixture is boiling and the sugar has dissolved, reduce the heat to low. Carefully add the roasted vermicelli back into the pot. Stir gently to ensure all the vermicelli is submerged in the milk.

instructions.title: Add Nuts and Simmer
instructions.text: Stir in half of the sliced almonds, pistachios, and raisins into the vermicelli mixture. Cover the pot with a lid and let it simmer on very low heat. The vermicelli will absorb the milk and become soft and tender.

instructions.title: Check for Doneness
instructions.text: After about 10-15 minutes, check if the vermicelli has absorbed most of the milk and is cooked through. It should be soft but not mushy. If it's too dry, you can add a tablespoon or two of hot water or milk. If it's too wet, cook uncovered for a few more minutes.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, turn off the heat and let the seviyan rest, covered, for 5 minutes. This allows the flavors to meld beautifully. Garnish with the remaining sliced almonds, pistachios, and raisins. Serve warm or chilled, as a delightful dessert or a sweet breakfast.

tips: For perfectly roasted vermicelli, keep the heat medium-low and stir constantly to prevent burning and ensure even browning. A golden color yields the best flavor.
Adjust the sugar level according to your preference; some like it sweeter, some less. You can also add a touch of condensed milk for extra creaminess.
Seviyan can be enjoyed warm as a comforting dessert or chilled for a refreshing treat. It stores well in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
article: Seviyan Vermicelli is a beloved Pakistani Pasta and Noodles dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
